label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me think about this step by step. The question is asking whether following a certain set of instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward, take 8 steps right, then take 8 steps left. The options are Yes or No. First, I need to visualize this. Let me imagine myself standing at a starting point. I'm facing forward, which I can assume is in a particular direction—let's say north for simplicity. Now, the first instruction is to take 8 steps to the right. When I'm facing north, my right is towards the east. So, taking 8 steps to the right would move me 8 units (steps) to the east. Let me mark that on a coordinate system. Let's say the starting point is (0,0). Facing north, right is east. So after 8 steps right, I would be at (8,0). Next, the instruction is to take 8 steps to the left. Now, I'm still facing north (since the instruction says to always face forward). So, my left when facing north is towards the west. Taking 8 steps to the left would move me 8 units west. Starting from (8,0), moving 8 steps west would bring me back to (0,0), the starting point. Wait a second. But I need to make sure I interpreted "right" and "left" correctly. When facing forward (north), right is east and left is west. So taking 8 steps to the right moves me east, then 8 steps to the left (west) brings me back. So yes, I would end up at the starting point. But let me double-check to make sure I haven't made any assumptions that might be incorrect. What if "right" and "left" refer to directions relative to my original facing direction throughout the entire process? The instruction says "always face forward," which I assume means I don't change direction during the process. So I stay facing forward (north) the entire time. Therefore, taking 8 steps right (east), then 8 steps left (west) would bring me back to the starting point. So the answer would be Yes. Another way to think about it is that moving right and then left by the same number of steps, while facing the same direction the entire time, cancels out the movement. So I end up where I started.
